channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Fly tipping on kids rock park, 29th June
item.description:

item.title: Behind black bins, 29th June
item.description:
Rubbish has been there weeks and getting worse

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Launcelot Road, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constantly overflowing / bin that creates more litter than it collects, 29th June
item.description:
I am writing to express my serious concern regarding the wholly inadequate open-topped litter bins situated at two locations in the Sydenham / Penge area: firstly, near the Tannsfeld Road entrance to Alexandra Recreation Ground, and secondly, at the Newlands Park bus stop (Penge East station end). The current arrangement is simply not fit for purpose, and I urge the Council to take immediate action to rectify this ongoing problem. The open-topped design of these bins, combined with the lack of a fixed base on Newlands Park, makes them entirely unsuitable for their respective locations. Foxes regularly access the bins during the night, scattering rubbish across the pavement and surrounding area. Furthermore, the bin on Newlands Park is easily tipped over, exacerbating the litter problem and creating an unsightly mess that residents are left to contend with each morning. This is unacceptable. I witnessed a particularly distressing scene today whilst heading for the train: an elderly lady, reliant upon a walking stick, was attempting to dispose of rubbish that had fallen from the toppled Newlands Park bin whilst simultaneously trying to drag it back to the bus stop from outside her sheltered housing building. When I stopped to help her, she confirmed this was a regular occurrence. The fact that this bin is situated directly outside a sheltered housing development compounds the issue, as it is the elderly and most vulnerable residents who bear the brunt of this problem. It is wholly unreasonable that vulnerable residents should be put in this position because the Council has failed to provide adequate street furniture.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Newlands Park, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kid's bed on pavement, 29th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mosslea Road, Penge, Greater London

item.title: 27 Buckley road, 29th June
item.description:
Most part of the street is very dirty. Broken bottles, general rubbish, used condom. The situation has been like this for a week.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Buckley Road, London

item.title: Bed frame on Brinkburn Close, next to lamp post, 29th June
item.description:
There's half a bed frame that's been left next to the lamp post on Brinkburn Close where the road meets Bracondale Road. It's been here for a few weeks now.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brinkburn Close, Abbey Wood

item.title: Lower shoots over pathway, 29th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Palace View, Widmore, Greater London

item.title: Multiple potholes outside driveway, 29th June
item.description:
Multiple potholes have formed along the edge of the road outside my property adjacent to the driveway access. The surface is continuing to deteriorate and the holes appear to be worsening each day. Water collects within the defects after rainfall, which is likely contributing to further breakdown of the road surface. The condition presents a potential hazard to vehicles and requires inspection and repair, plus repainting of yellow line.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 88, Durham Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ashburnham Grove, Greenwich
